stem: The measure of angle R is 2π/3 radians. The measure of angle T is 5π/12 radians greater than the measure of angle R. What is the measure of angle T, in degrees?
explanation: Angle T = 2π/3 + 5π/12 = 8π/12 + 5π/12 = 13π/12 radians. Converting to degrees by multiplying by 180/π gives (13/12)(180) = 195 degrees.
